structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Status Mismatch Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan alarm when status differs from command for 15 seconds. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2; commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Status mismatch for 15 seconds
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Raise corresponding alarm level
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Commanded ON, status OFF; or commanded OFF, status ON for 15 s
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds.
a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2
b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4

evidence_quote:
Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds.
a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2
b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4

a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2
b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4
judge_reason: Fan status mismatch alarm rule, clearly stated in evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Evaluation Based on OS
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault conditions not applicable to the current Operating State shall not be evaluated.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Current OS change
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Skip evaluation of inapplicable fault conditions
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fault conditions  that are not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated.

evidence_quote: Fault conditions  that are not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated.
evidence_text: Fault conditions  that are not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated.
judge_reason: AFDD fault evaluation based on OS, directly from evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Alarm Delay
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition that evaluates as TRUE must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before being reported to the operator.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fault condition TRUE
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Wait AlarmDelay minutes then report
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: A fault condition  that evaluates as TRUE  must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.

evidence_quote: A fault condition  that evaluates as TRUE  must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.
evidence_text: A fault condition  that evaluates as TRUE  must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.
judge_reason: AFDD fault alarm delay rule, clearly stated in evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Evaluation Suspension Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: AFDD fault condition evaluation suspended when AHU not operating, and for ModeDelay minutes after a change in mode of any Zone Group served.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: AHU off or mode change of Zone Group
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suspend evaluation for specified time
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Evaluation of fault conditions  shall be suspended under the following conditions:
a. When AHU is not operating
b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode  (e.g., from Warmup  Mode to Occupied Mode ) of any Zone Group  served by the AHU

evidence_quote:
Evaluation of fault conditions  shall be suspended under the following conditions:
a. When AHU is not operating
b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode  (e.g., from Warmup  Mode to Occupied Mode ) of any Zone Group  served by the AHU
evidence_text:
Evaluation of fault conditions  shall be suspended under the following conditions:
a. When AHU is not operating
b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode  (e.g., from Warmup  Mode to Occupied Mode ) of any Zone Group  served by the AHU
judge_reason: AFDD evaluation suspension conditions, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Maintenance Interval Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fan runtime > 1500 hours
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Raise Level 4 alarm; reset counter on acknowledge
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4.  Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.

evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4.  Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
evidence_text: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4.  Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
judge_reason: Fan maintenance interval alarm rule, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Fan Run for Non-Unoccupied Modes
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Unit mode not Unoccupied
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Supply fan runs
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode .

evidence_quote: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode .
evidence_text: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode .
judge_reason: Operational rule for supply fan running, clearly grounded in ASHRAE Guideline 36.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Medium Fan Speed Reset Based on Outdoor Air Temperature
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Medium fan speed MedSpeed reset linearly from MinSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed based on outdoor air temperature relative to Endpoint #1 and Endpoint #2.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: OAT compared to endpoints
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: MedSpeed varies linearly between MinSpeed and MaxCoolSpeed
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
b. Medium fan speed MedSpeed  shall be reset linearly based on outdoor air temperature from MinSpeed when outdoor air temperature is greater or equal to Endpoint #1 to MaxCoolSpeed when outdoor air temperature is less than or equal to Endpoint #2.
1. Endpoint #1: the lesser of zone temperature +0.5°C (1°F) and maximum supply air dew point MaxDPT.
2. Endpoint #2: the lesser of zone temperature minus 6°C (10°F) and the maximum supply air dew point MaxDPT minus 1°C (2°F).

evidence_quote:
b. Medium fan speed MedSpeed  shall be reset linearly based on outdoor air temperature from MinSpeed when outdoor air temperature is greater or equal to Endpoint #1 to MaxCoolSpeed when outdoor air temperature is less than or equal to Endpoint #2.
1. Endpoint #1: the lesser of zone temperature +0.5°C (1°F) and maximum supply air dew point MaxDPT.
2. Endpoint #2: the lesser of zone temperature minus 6°C (10°F) and the maximum supply air dew point MaxDPT minus 1°C (2°F).
evidence_text:
b. Medium fan speed MedSpeed  shall be reset linearly based on outdoor air temperature from MinSpeed when outdoor air temperature is greater or equal to Endpoint #1 to MaxCoolSpeed when outdoor air temperature is less than or equal to Endpoint #2.
1. Endpoint #1: the lesser of zone temperature +0.5°C (1°F) and maximum supply air dew point MaxDPT.
2. Endpoint #2: the lesser of zone temperature minus 6°C (10°F) and the maximum supply air dew point MaxDPT minus 1°C (2°F).
judge_reason: Detailed operational sequence for medium fan speed reset based on OAT, supported by quote.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Air Temperature Setpoint Deadband
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Deadband values of SATsp and SATsp-C shall be the average of zone heating and cooling setpoints, limited to no lower than 21°C (70°F) and no higher than 24°C (75°F).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set SATsp and SATsp-C deadband within limits
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. The Deadband values of SATsp and SATsp -C shall be the average of the zone heating setpoint and the zone cooling setpoint  but shall be no lower than 21°C (70°F) and no higher than 24°C (75°F).

evidence_quote: a. The Deadband values of SATsp and SATsp -C shall be the average of the zone heating setpoint and the zone cooling setpoint  but shall be no lower than 21°C (70°F) and no higher than 24°C (75°F).
evidence_text: a. The Deadband values of SATsp and SATsp -C shall be the average of the zone heating setpoint and the zone cooling setpoint  but shall be no lower than 21°C (70°F) and no higher than 24°C (75°F).
judge_reason: Operational rule for supply air temperature setpoint deadband, directly from evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Fan Speed Ramp Limit
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan speed changes limited to no more than 10% per minute.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.

evidence_quote: Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.
evidence_text: Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.
judge_reason: Parameter spec for fan speed ramp limit, directly from evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Fan Speed Mapping from Heating/Cooling Loop Signals
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan speed setpoint determined from heating and cooling loop signals as per defined breakpoints: heating loop 100-50% → MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed; 50-0% → MinSpeed; deadband → MinSpeed; cooling loop 0-25% → MinSpeed; 25-50% → MinSpeed to MedSpeed; 50-75% → MedSpeed; 75-100% → MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Heating or cooling loop signal value
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set fan speed according to signal range as described
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. For a heating -loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed.
b. For a heating -loop signal of 50% to 0%, fan speed setpoint  is MinSpeed.
c. In deadband, fan speed setpoint  is MinSpeed.
d. For a cooling -loop signal of 0% to 25%, fan speed is MinSpeed.
e. For a cooling -loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to Med Speed.
f. For a cooling -loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed.
g. For a cooling -loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.

evidence_quote:
a. For a heating -loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed.
b. For a heating -loop signal of 50% to 0%, fan speed setpoint  is MinSpeed.
c. In deadband, fan speed setpoint  is MinSpeed.
d. For a cooling -loop signal of 0% to 25%, fan speed is MinSpeed.
e. For a cooling -loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to Med Speed.
f. For a cooling -loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed.
g. For a cooling -loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.
evidence_text:
a. For a heating -loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed.
b. For a heating -loop signal of 50% to 0%, fan speed setpoint  is MinSpeed.
c. In deadband, fan speed setpoint  is MinSpeed.
d. For a cooling -loop signal of 0% to 25%, fan speed is MinSpeed.
e. For a cooling -loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to Med Speed.
f. For a cooling -loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed.
g. For a cooling -loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.
judge_reason: Operational mapping from heating/cooling loops to fan speed, fully supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Coil Control Loop Enable
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Control loop for SATsp-C enabled when supply fan proven ON and Zone State is cooling; otherwise disabled and set to neutral. When enabled, modulates cooling coil to control SAT to SATsp-C.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan proven ON and Zone State = cooling
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable control loop; modulate cooling coil to maintain SATsp-C
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The control loop for SATsp -C is enabled when the supply fan is proven ON and the Zone State  is cooling and disabled and set to neutral otherwise. When enabled, supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp -C by modulating the cooling coil.

evidence_quote: The control loop for SATsp -C is enabled when the supply fan is proven ON and the Zone State  is cooling and disabled and set to neutral otherwise. When enabled, supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp -C by modulating the cooling coil.
evidence_text: The control loop for SATsp -C is enabled when the supply fan is proven ON and the Zone State  is cooling and disabled and set to neutral otherwise. When enabled, supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp -C by modulating the cooling coil.
judge_reason: Cooling coil control loop enable/disable logic, directly from evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Minimum Outdoor Air Damper Control without OA Flow Measurement - MinOA-P Determination
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If MinOAsp is zero, MinOA-P shall be zero. If nonzero, MinOA-P set as a value between MinPos* and DesPos* proportional to MinOAsp relative to MinOA and DesOA; MinPos* and DesPos* are calculated from fan speed each minute.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: MinOAsp value
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set MinOA-P to 0 if MinOAsp=0, else interpolate between MinPos* and DesPos*
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
d. If MinOAsp is zero, MinOA -P shall be zero (i.e., outdoor air damper fully closed).
e. If MinOAsp is nonzero, then the outdoor air damper minimum position MinOA -P shall be the value between MinPos* and DesPos* that is proportional to the value of MinOAsp between MinOA and DesOA.

evidence_quote:
d. If MinOAsp is zero, MinOA -P shall be zero (i.e., outdoor air damper fully closed).
e. If MinOAsp is nonzero, then the outdoor air damper minimum position MinOA -P shall be the value between MinPos* and DesPos* that is proportional to the value of MinOAsp between MinOA and DesOA.
evidence_text:
d. If MinOAsp is zero, MinOA -P shall be zero (i.e., outdoor air damper fully closed).
e. If MinOAsp is nonzero, then the outdoor air damper minimum position MinOA -P shall be the value between MinPos* and DesPos* that is proportional to the value of MinOAsp between MinOA and DesOA.
judge_reason: Operational rule for minimum outdoor air damper without flow measurement, supported by quote.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Minimum Outdoor Air Damper Control with OA Flow Measurement
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Minimum outdoor airflow maintained at MinOAsp by reverse-acting control loop modulating MinOA-P, enabled only when supply fan proven on and in Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan proven ON and Occupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable loop; control OA flow to setpoint by modulating MinOA-P
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. Minimum outdoor air control loop is enabled when the supply fan is proven on and in Occupied Mode  and disabled and output set to zero otherwise.
b. The minimum outdoor airflow rate shall be maintained at the minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp by a reverse -acting control loop whose output is mapped to MinOA -P.

evidence_quote:
a. Minimum outdoor air control loop is enabled when the supply fan is proven on and in Occupied Mode  and disabled and output set to zero otherwise.
b. The minimum outdoor airflow rate shall be maintained at the minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp by a reverse -acting control loop whose output is mapped to MinOA -P.
evidence_text:
a. Minimum outdoor air control loop is enabled when the supply fan is proven on and in Occupied Mode  and disabled and output set to zero otherwise.
b. The minimum outdoor airflow rate shall be maintained at the minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp by a reverse -acting control loop whose output is mapped to MinOA -P.
judge_reason: Operational rule for minimum OA control with flow measurement, directly from evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Economizer Disable/Re-enable Delay
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Once the economizer is disabled, it shall not be reenabled within 10 minutes, and vice versa.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Economizer disable or enable event
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enforce 10-minute minimum off/on time
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Once the economizer is disabled, it shall not be r eenabled within 10 minutes and vice versa.

evidence_quote: Once the economizer is disabled, it shall not be r eenabled within 10 minutes and vice versa.
evidence_text: Once the economizer is disabled, it shall not be r eenabled within 10 minutes and vice versa.
judge_reason: Economizer disable/re-enable delay rule, clearly stated in evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Economizer Damper Maximum Position Lockout
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When economizer enabled, MaxOA-P = 100%. When disabled, set MaxOA-P equal to MinOA-P.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Economizer enabled/disabled state
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set MaxOA-P accordingly
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When economizer is enabled, MaxOA -P = 100%. When economizer is disabled, set MaxOA -P equal to MinOA -P.

evidence_quote: When economizer is enabled, MaxOA -P = 100%. When economizer is disabled, set MaxOA -P equal to MinOA -P.
evidence_text: When economizer is enabled, MaxOA -P = 100%. When economizer is disabled, set MaxOA -P equal to MinOA -P.
judge_reason: Economizer damper max position lockout logic, directly from evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Exhaust Damper Interlock with Supply and Return Fans
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Exhaust damper shall open whenever associated supply fan and return fan are proven ON, and shall be closed otherwise.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan and return fan both proven ON
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Open exhaust damper; close otherwise
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. Exhaust damper shall open whenever associated supply fan and return fan are proven ON and shall be closed otherwise .

evidence_quote: a. Exhaust damper shall open whenever associated supply fan and return fan are proven ON and shall be closed otherwise .
evidence_text: a. Exhaust damper shall open whenever associated supply fan and return fan are proven ON and shall be closed otherwise .
judge_reason: Exhaust damper interlock rule, directly from evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Return Fan Run Interlock
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Return fan shall run whenever associated supply fan is proven ON.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan proven ON
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Run return fan
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. Return fan shall run whenever associated supply fan is proven ON.

evidence_quote: b. Return fan shall run whenever associated supply fan is proven ON.
evidence_text: b. Return fan shall run whenever associated supply fan is proven ON.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Return Fan Speed Tracking Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Return fan speed controlled to maintain speed equal to supply fan speed less differential S-R-SPD-DIFF*, where S-R-SPD-DIFF* = S-R-SPD-DIFF * (MinOAsp / DesOA).
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Return fan running
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Modulate return fan speed to track supply fan speed minus adjusted differential
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: d. Return -fan speed shall b e the  controlled to maintain return fan speed equal to supply fan speed less differential  S-R-SPD-DIFF*.

evidence_quote: d. Return -fan speed shall b e the  controlled to maintain return fan speed equal to supply fan speed less differential  S-R-SPD-DIFF*.
evidence_text: d. Return -fan speed shall b e the  controlled to maintain return fan speed equal to supply fan speed less differential  S-R-SPD-DIFF*.
judge_reason: Return fan speed tracking control rule, supported by evidence with equation.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Relief Dampers Direct Building Pressure Control
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Control of Actuated Relief Dampers without Fans
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Relief dampers enabled when supply fan proven ON; when enabled, P-only control modulates to maintain 12 Pa (0.05 in. water) building static pressure; close when disabled.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan proven ON
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable dampers; modulate to maintain 12 Pa
configurable_values: 12 Pa (0.05 in. water) static pressure setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. Relief dampers shall be enabled when the associated supply fan is proven ON, and disabled otherwise.
b. When enabled, use a P -only control loop to modulate relief dampers to maintain 12 Pa ( 0.05 in. of water)  building static pressure. Close damper when disabled.

evidence_quote:
a. Relief dampers shall be enabled when the associated supply fan is proven ON, and disabled otherwise.
b. When enabled, use a P -only control loop to modulate relief dampers to maintain 12 Pa ( 0.05 in. of water)  building static pressure. Close damper when disabled.
evidence_text:
a. Relief dampers shall be enabled when the associated supply fan is proven ON, and disabled otherwise.
b. When enabled, use a P -only control loop to modulate relief dampers to maintain 12 Pa ( 0.05 in. of water)  building static pressure. Close damper when disabled.
judge_reason: Relief dampers direct pressure control sequence, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: First-Stage Freeze Protection
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SAT drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send heating plant requests, override OA damper to minimum, and modulate heating coil to maintain SAT ≥ 6°C (42°F). Disable when SAT rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: SAT < 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: 1. Send HHW plant requests; 2. Override OA damper to minimum; 3. Modulate heating coil to maintain SAT ≥ 6°C (42°F)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot -water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5  minutes.

evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot -water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5  minutes.
evidence_text: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot -water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5  minutes.
judge_reason: First-stage freeze protection logic, fully detailed in evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Third-Stage Freeze Protection Shutdown
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon freeze-stat signal, or if SAT < 3°C (38°F) for 15 min or < 1°C (34°F) for 5 min, shut down fans, close OA damper, set cooling coil valve to 100%, energize CHW pumps, send HHW plant requests, modulate heating coil to maintain SAT or MAT at 27°C (80°F), and set Level 2 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Freeze-stat signal, or SAT < 3°C for 15 min, or SAT < 1°C for 5 min
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: 1. Shut down supply and return/relief fans; 2. Close OA damper; 3. Set cooling coil valve to 100%; 4. Energize CHW pump system; 5. Send HHW plant requests; 6. Modulate heating coil to maintain SAT or MAT at 27°C (80°F); 7. Set Level 2 alarm
configurable_values: SAT thresholds: 3°C (38°F) for 15 min, 1°C (34°F) for 5 min
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Upon signal from the freeze -stat (if installed), or if supply air temperature dro ps below 3°C (38°F) for 15 minutes or below 1°C (34°F) for 5 minutes, shut down supply and return/relief fan(s), close outdoor air damper, make the minimum cooling -coil valve position 100%, and energize the CHW pump system. Send two (or more, as required t o ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot -water plant requests, modulate the heating coil to maintain the higher of the supply air temperature or the mixed air temperature at 27°C (80°F), and set a Level 2 alarm indicating the unit is shut down by  freeze protection.

evidence_quote: Upon signal from the freeze -stat (if installed), or if supply air temperature dro ps below 3°C (38°F) for 15 minutes or below 1°C (34°F) for 5 minutes, shut down supply and return/relief fan(s), close outdoor air damper, make the minimum cooling -coil valve position 100%, and energize the CHW pump system. Send two (or more, as required t o ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot -water plant requests, modulate the heating coil to maintain the higher of the supply air temperature or the mixed air temperature at 27°C (80°F), and set a Level 2 alarm indicating the unit is shut down by  freeze protection.
evidence_text: Upon signal from the freeze -stat (if installed), or if supply air temperature dro ps below 3°C (38°F) for 15 minutes or below 1°C (34°F) for 5 minutes, shut down supply and return/relief fan(s), close outdoor air damper, make the minimum cooling -coil valve position 100%, and energize the CHW pump system. Send two (or more, as required t o ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot -water plant requests, modulate the heating coil to maintain the higher of the supply air temperature or the mixed air temperature at 27°C (80°F), and set a Level 2 alarm indicating the unit is shut down by  freeze protection.
judge_reason: Third-stage freeze protection shutdown, full details in evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Freeze Protection Shutdown Reset Condition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If freeze protection shutdown triggered by low air temperature sensor, it remains until reset by software switch from operator's workstation. If a freeze-stat with physical reset switch used, no software reset.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Shutdown triggered by low air temperature sensor
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Require operator reset unless physical freeze-stat used
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If a freeze protection shutdown is triggered by a low air temperature sensor reading, it shall remain in effect until it is reset by a software switch from the operator’s workstation. (If a freeze stat with a physical reset switch is us ed instead, there shall be no software reset switch).

evidence_quote: a. If a freeze protection shutdown is triggered by a low air temperature sensor reading, it shall remain in effect until it is reset by a software switch from the operator’s workstation. (If a freeze stat with a physical reset switch is us ed instead, there shall be no software reset switch).
evidence_text: a. If a freeze protection shutdown is triggered by a low air temperature sensor reading, it shall remain in effect until it is reset by a software switch from the operator’s workstation. (If a freeze stat with a physical reset switch is us ed instead, there shall be no software reset switch).
judge_reason: Freeze protection reset condition rule, directly from evidence.
